name: Search Bar
description: Drop-in InlineSearch/SearchOverlay for app search; use instead of raw SearchInput when showing or filtering results
details: Use this before hand-rolling search over local arrays, feeds, tables, pickers, or RecordRoom records. Installs a component-only search toolkit with lightweight InlineSearch + useSearchIndex for simple in-page filtering, plus SearchOverlay for global/command search, keyboard navigation, grouped results, loading/error/empty states, click selection, optional Enter-to-submit query navigation, opt-in match highlighting, overlay-only recent result and query searches, and a debounced async-search helper. It does not add a route or demo page; import the installed component into the page you are building.
instructions: Installs src/components/search/SearchOverlay.tsx (exports InlineSearch, SearchOverlay, and useSearchIndex). It adds no route — import what you need into your own page.
Use InlineSearch + useSearchIndex for in-page filtering (lists, feeds, tables, pickers, RecordRoom records); use SearchOverlay for global/command-style search.
Use the raw SearchInput primitive only for a bare input with no result list, ranking, keyboard navigation, or selection handling.
agentNotes: Use InlineSearch when search belongs to a specific page section and results should appear beneath that input; use SearchOverlay when search is temporary, global, command-like, includes recent searches, submits partial text to a search-results page, or should interrupt the current workflow.
Pass onSearchSubmit when pressing Enter should navigate to or render a full search-results page for the typed query; result clicks still call onSelect.
Ask the user which placement they prefer only when both overlay and inline search are plausible and the page context does not make the placement obvious.

Use SearchOverlay's recentStorageKey, maxRecentItems, and showRecentSearches props when recent selections or submitted text queries should appear before the user types; keep recent searches off inline controls unless the surrounding UI has room for them.

Use getHighlightedParts with renderTitle, renderSubtitle, or renderDescription when matching text should be highlighted; leave highlighting to app-specific renderers so colors, emphasis, and density match the local design.
patterns: For local feed/list filtering, keep the source array in the page, derive results with useSearchIndex, map results to SearchItem[], and render InlineSearch where the user expects to search.
For RecordRoom data, call useQuery in the page and pass records mapped to SearchItem[] into InlineSearch or SearchOverlay.

For overlay search that previews matching items but routes Enter to a results page, pass onSearchSubmit={(query) => navigate(`/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}`)} and keep onSelect for direct result clicks.
Keep selections app-specific: create or update records in the onSelect handler only after the user explicitly chooses a result.
Use mocked or debounced async providers during prototyping before wiring live integration calls.
For privacy-sensitive search contexts, set showRecentSearches={false} or use a scoped recentStorageKey per page or account context.
